source_jobs.title: Mobile Developer
source_jobs.description:
About Knowtex

Knowtex is building the future of voice AI operating systems for clinicians, transforming how healthcare documentation happens at the point of care. Founded by Stanford AI scientists with deep clinical experience, we're experiencing explosive growth across both commercial health systems and federal healthcare, with our ambient documentation platform scaling rapidly to thousands of clinicians across hundreds of specialties. We're at an inflection point where cutting-edge AI meets real clinical impact, giving clinicians hours back each day to focus on what matters most - their patients.

Position Overview

We are seeking a Mobile Developer to help build and scale clinician-facing mobile applications that extend Knowtex’s ambient documentation platform into real-world care environments. This role focuses on delivering performant, intuitive mobile experiences that integrate seamlessly with our AI-powered backend systems.

Key Responsibilities

 - Develop and maintain iOS and/or Android mobile applications

 - Integrate mobile clients with backend APIs and real-time AI services

 - Optimize performance, reliability, and offline capabilities

 - Collaborate with frontend and backend engineers on API design

 - Implement secure authentication and HIPAA-compliant data handling

 - Participate in release cycles and app store deployment processes

Required Qualifications

 - 3–6+ years of mobile development experience

 - Experience with Swift, Kotlin, React Native, or Flutter

 - Strong understanding of API integration and mobile networking

 - Experience with mobile performance optimization

 - Familiarity with secure mobile app architecture

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience in healthcare applications

 - Experience integrating AI-driven features in mobile apps

 - Experience building enterprise-grade mobile apps

Technical Environment

 - Swift / Kotlin / React Native / Flutter

 - RESTful APIs

 - AWS backend services

 - OAuth2 / secure authentication flows

Compensation & Benefits

 - Meaningful equity compensation

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Premium health, dental, and vision coverage

 - 401(k) plan

source_jobs.title: Founding Technical Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
Founding Technical Product Manager

Knowtex • In-Person/Hybrid (San Francisco)




ABOUT KNOWTEX

Knowtex is building the clinical intelligence platform for clinicians, transforming the way medicine is practiced, and how patient care is completed. Founded by Stanford AI scientists with deep federal, clinical, and technical experience, we’re experiencing explosive growth across both commercial health systems and government healthcare. Our ambient documentation platform scales to thousands of clinicians across hundreds of specialties, and we’re serving clinicians across the country. We’re at an inflection point where cutting-edge AI meets real clinical impact—giving clinicians hours back each day to focus on what matters most: their patients.


POSITION OVERVIEW

Knowtex is hiring a founding Technical Product Manager to own critical product areas at the intersection of AI, clinical workflows, and healthcare infrastructure. These roles require strong technical depth, the ability to work directly with clinicians and healthcare administrators to understand complex workflows, and the skill to translate those insights into clear engineering requirements and product strategy.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Define product roadmap, prioritize features, and drive execution for your product area

 - Conduct discovery with clinicians, healthcare administrators, and IT leaders to deeply understand clinical workflows and pain points

 - Translate complex clinical and operational requirements into clear engineering specs, user stories, and acceptance criteria

 - Partner closely with engineering, AI/ML, and design teams to ship high-quality products on tight timelines

 - Own product metrics, conduct data analysis, and use quantitative and qualitative signals to drive decisions

 - Manage cross-functional stakeholders including clinical operations, compliance, sales, and customer success

 - Evaluate build vs. buy vs. partner decisions for integrations and capabilities

 - Communicate product vision and progress to leadership, customers, and external partners


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 2+ years of product management experience with a strong technical foundation

 - Ability to read and reason about code, APIs, data models, and system architecture

 - Experience working directly with clinicians, healthcare administrators, or clinical operations teams

 - Demonstrated ability to distill ambiguous, complex stakeholder needs into actionable engineering requirements

 - Strong analytical skills—comfortable with SQL, product analytics tools, and data-driven decision making

 - Excellent written and verbal communication; ability to present to both technical and non-technical audiences

 - Experience working in fast-paced startup environments with high ownership and autonomy

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field (or equivalent experience)


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

Government & Federal Healthcare:

 - Experience with federal healthcare (VA, DoD, MHS) or government contracting

 - Familiarity with FedRAMP, ATO processes, IL4/IL5 compliance, or FISMA

 - Experience managing enterprise-scale onboarding and change management programs

 - Understanding of federal procurement processes and stakeholder dynamics



Clinical Automation:

 - Experience with EMR/EHR platforms (Epic, Cerner, OncoEMR) or healthcare interoperability standards (FHIR, HL7)

 - Background in clinical documentation, medical coding (CPT, ICD-10), or revenue cycle management

 - Experience with NLP/AI products, particularly in accuracy measurement and hallucination reduction

 - Understanding of clinical workflow design across medical specialties



Clinical Intelligence:

 - Experience with clinical decision support, clinical trial matching, or evidence-based guidelines (NCCN, ASCO)

 - Background in health informatics, biomedical data, or life sciences technology

 - Experience building AI/ML-powered products, chatbots, or knowledge retrie

source_jobs.title: Join Our Talent Community
source_jobs.description:
Join our talent community! As we continue to grow and build toward our mission, we’re always excited to connect with thoughtful, mission-driven problem solvers across a range of disciplines.

If you don’t see an open role that feels like the right fit today, we’d still love to hear from you. Tell us what excites you about Knowtex, the type of role where you believe you’d thrive, and anything else you’d like us to know about you.

As new opportunities open up that align with your skills and experience, we’ll reach out to explore next steps.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer (Applications Engineering)
source_jobs.description:
Position Overview

We are seeking a frontend-leaning engineer to help build and scale the user-facing web applications powering Knowtex’s AI-driven clinical documentation platform. You will work closely with Platform Engineering and customer-facing teams to build accessible, intuitive, and performant user experiences that support real-time voice AI workflows across enterprise healthcare environments.

This role is ideal for an engineer who has experience operating on small, high-impact teams with significant ownership and autonomy, including end-to-end execution on critical product features.

Key Responsibilities

 - Exercise strong engineering judgement in evolving or breaking from existing patterns when appropriate

 - Design and build user experiences, including complex audio-centric workflows

 - Develop accessible, intuitive, and performant user interfaces

 - Develop secure and performant APIs and supporting infrastructure for web and mobile applications

 - Collaborate closely with engineering, product, and customer delivery teams to prioritize bug fixes, improvements, and new feature development

 - Participate meaningfully in technical discussions, design reviews, and code reviews

 - Contribute to shared application infrastructure, component library, design system, and developer experience

 - Improve system reliability, observability, and performance

 - Handle engineering escalations from customer-facing teams during on-call shifts, including investigating issues outside of your typical areas of responsibility

Required Qualifications

 - 3+ years of experience building production web applications

 - Strong proficiency in TypeScript

 - Experience building applications with React and Node.js

 - Experience designing and building RESTful APIs

 - Experience working with relational and/or NoSQL databases

 - Understanding of distributed systems and scalability concepts

 - Strong debugging, communication, and problem-solving skills

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience in healthcare technology or regulated environments

 - Experience building real-time or highly interactive web applications

 - Experience operating effectively with limited product design support

 - Familiarity with AWS services such as Lambda, ECS, S3, RDS, and DynamoDB

Technical Environment

 - React, TypeScript, Node.js, and Python (Lambda)

 - AWS cloud infrastructure (including GovCloud environments)

 - AI-enabled application platforms

 - CI/CD pipelines and containerized services

 - Observability and analytics tools (Amplitude, Sentry)

Compensation & Benefits

 - Meaningful equity compensation

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Premium health, dental, and vision coverage

 - 401(k) plan

notes: Knowtex is a clinical intelligence platform for clinicians, with a strong focus on AI and engineering.
